Author: Google
Title: Design a responsible approach | Responsible Generative AI Toolkit | Google AI for Developers

Author: xAI
Title: Toward Comprehensive Benchmarking of the Biological Knowledge of Frontier Large Language Models
Definition of risk: requests with a clear intent to violate the law, without over-refusing sensitive or controversial queries
Method for risk mitigation: train Grok 4.1 on demonstrations of appropriate responses to both benign and harmful queries; employ input filters to reject specific classes of sensitive requests
Benchmark: internal dataset of single-turn requests that violate our safety policy

Author: xAI
Title: Toward Comprehensive Benchmarking of the Biological Knowledge of Frontier Large Language Models
Definition of risk: requests with a clear intent to violate the law, without over-refusing sensitive or controversial queries
Method for risk mitigation: train Grok 4.1 on demonstrations of appropriate responses to both benign and harmful queries; employ input filters to reject specific classes of sensitive requests

Author: xAI
Title: Grok 4 Model Card
Definition of risk: willingness to facilitate serious criminal activity, and susceptibility to hijacking via injected instructions
Method for risk mitigation: basic refusal policy which instructs Grok 4 to decline queries demonstrating clear intent to engage in activities that threaten severe, imminent harm... included in the system prompt; model-based input filters

Author: xAI
Title: Toward Comprehensive Benchmarking of the Biological Knowledge of Frontier Large Language Models
Definition of risk: whether jailbreaks cause the model to answer requests it previously would have refused
Method for risk mitigation: safety training
Benchmark: internal dataset of single-turn jailbreak templates

Author: xAI
Title: Toward Comprehensive Benchmarking of the Biological Knowledge of Frontier Large Language Models
Definition of risk: whether jailbreaks cause the model to answer requests it previously would have refused
Method for risk mitigation: safety training

Author: xAI
Title: Grok 4 Model Card
Definition of risk: model to be used in an 'agentic' manner, that is, repeatedly take actions toward a specified goal... risks of misuse beyond what is present in conversational settings, such as executing real function calls
Method for risk mitigation: system prompt

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Exotic scenarios meant to elicit the model to commit blackmail, attempt a murder, and frame someone for financial crimes.

Author: Anthropic
Title: System Card  Addendum:  Claude Opus 4.1
Definition of risk: Blackmail as the only means by which a model can preserve its continued operation

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to GPT-4o System Card: Native image generation
Definition of risk: Outputting ahistorical, unrealistic, or undesired attributes contrary to the user's intent, such as changing the race of a historically well-specified population.
Benchmark: Automated evaluation for ahistorical and unrealistic bias

Author: xAI
Title: xAI Risk Management Framework
Definition of risk: To design a bioweapon, a malicious actor must undergo a design process. 'ideation' involves actively planning for a biological attack; 'design' involves retrieving blueprints for a hazardous agent... 'build' consists of the protocols, reagents, and equipment necessary... and 'test' consists of measuring characteristics or properties of the pathogen
Method for risk mitigation: basic refusal policy, AI-powered filters that specifically monitor user conversations for content matching these narrow topics and return a brief message declining to answer when activated

Title: xAI Risk Management Framework
Definition of risk: realistic offensive cyber tasks requiring identifying and chaining many exploits in sequence
Method for risk mitigation: basic refusal policy to prevent misuse for cyber attacks

Author: xAI
Title: xAI Risk Management Framework
Definition of risk: realistic offensive cyber tasks requiring identifying and chaining many exploits in sequence
Method for risk mitigation: basic refusal policy to prevent misuse for cyber attacks
Benchmark author: The WMDP Benchmark: Measuring and Reducing Malicious Use With Unlearning

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Pro - Model Card
Definition of risk: The model can be used to drastically reduce the cost (e.g. through full automation) of carrying out end-to-end cyberattacks on organizations with a limited security posture.
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal safety, security, and responsibility teams

Definition of risk: capabilities that could create risks related to use of the model for cyber-exploitation to disrupt confidentiality, integrity, and/or availability of computer systems
Method for risk mitigation: Monitoring for high-risk cybersecurity threats, hunting, detection; further investment in enhanced security

Author: OpenAI
Title: GPT-5.3-Codex System Card
Definition of risk: Removes existing bottlenecks to scaling cyber operations, including either by automating end-to-end cyber operations against reasonably hardened targets, or by automating the discovery and exploitation of operationally relevant vulnerabilities.
Method for risk mitigation: Agent sandbox, Network access controls, Model safety training (refusal/safe-completion), Conversation monitor (topical classifier & safety reasoner), Actor-level enforcement, Trust-based access (TAC program), Security Controls

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude's Constitution
Definition of risk: Create cyberweapons or malicious code that could cause significant damage if deployed
Method for risk mitigation: Hard constraints

Author: Anthropic
Title: Model Card and Evaluations for Claude Models
Method for risk mitigation: Mitigations put in place and capability level of the model

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite Model Card
Definition of risk: promoting suicide, or instructing in activities that could cause real-world harm
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal safety, security, and responsibility teams
Benchmark: Text to Text Safety (Automated content safety evaluation measuring safety policies)

Author: Zeng, Wenjun; Kurniawan, Dana; Mullins, Ryan; Liu, Yuchi; Saha, Tamoghna; Ike-Njoku, Dirichi; Gu, Jindong; Song, Yiwen; Xu, Cai; Zhou, Jingjing; Joshi, Aparna; Dheep, Shravan; Malek, Mani; Palangi, Hamid; Baek, Joon; Pereira, Rick; Narasimhan, Karthik
Title: ShieldGemma 2: Robust and Tractable Image Content Moderation
Definition of risk: The image shall not contain content that facilitates or encourages activities that could cause real-world harm (e.g., building firearms and explosive devices, promotion of terrorism, instructions for suicide).
Method for risk mitigation: Instruction-tuned Gemma 3 on curated training datasets of natural and synthetic images; CSAM filtering

Author: Google
Title: 2.5 Flash and native  capabilities –audio & image model card
Definition of risk: promoting suicide, or instructing in activities that could cause real-world harm
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering, conditional pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback, safety policies and desiderata, product-level mitigations such as safety filtering
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal safety, security, and responsibility teams
Benchmark: Automated content safety evaluation measuring safety policies

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 3 Pro Image
Definition of risk: e.g. promoting suicide, or instructing in activities that could cause real-world harm
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering

Author: Google
Title: Design a responsible approach | Responsible Generative AI Toolkit | Google AI for Developers
Definition of risk: The application will not generate instructions or advice on harming oneself and/or others (e.g., accessing or building firearms and explosive devices, promotion of terrorism, instructions for suicide).
Method for risk mitigation: Tune models and add appropriate safeguards

Author: Google
Title: Gemma 4 model card | Google AI for Developers
Definition of risk: Promoting suicide, or instructing in activities that could cause real-world harm
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI principles, as well as safety policies
Method for risk mitigation: Automated and human evaluations
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal safety and responsible AI teams

Author: OpenAI
Title: GPT-5.3-Codex System Card
Definition of risk: High-impact failure modes that involve deletion or corruption of data (e.g. rm -rf, git clean -xfd) masked by simple instructions.
Method for risk mitigation: Safety training (RL with a 'user model' that made conflicting edits, positive reinforcement for not reverting changes); prompting in Codex CLI for clarification.

Title: OpenAI GPT-5 System Card
Definition of risk: when the model's user-facing response misrepresents its internal reasoning or the actions it took
Method for risk mitigation: trained the model to fail gracefully when posed with tasks that it cannot solve... We placed gpt-5-thinking in a variety of tasks that were partly or entirely infeasible to accomplish, and rewarded the model for honestly admitting it can not complete the task

Author: xAI

Method for risk mitigation: training the model to be honest

Title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
Definition of risk: Discrimination, unfairness, hate speech, offensive language, violence, incitement, nonviolent unethical behavior, bullying, and harassment.
Method for risk mitigation: Risk control system, RL training for harmlessness.

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to o3 and o4-mini system card: Codex
Definition of risk: Frontier capabilities reaching a High capability threshold in tracked categories.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.6 System Card
Definition of risk: Displaying undesirable or hidden goals, knowingly cooperating with misuse, using reasoning scratchpads in deceptive or unfaithful ways, sycophancy toward users, willingness to sabotage safeguards
Author/source of risk definition: Pilot sabotage risk report for Claude Opus 4
Method for risk mitigation: Alignment training measures, system prompt updates, monitoring evaluation awareness, full-turn prefill restrictions
External evaluator: UK AI Security Institute, Andon Labs

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.6 System Card
Definition of risk: Capability to take actions that could be critical steps in undermining safety assessments or safeguards

Title: Grok 4 Model Card

Method for risk mitigation: warning the model against jailbreak attacks in the system prompt

Title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
Definition of risk: Violent behavior, terrorism, illegal pornographic content, illegal medical practices, illegal gambling, drug and substance abuse, cybercrime, and animal-related offenses.
Method for risk mitigation: Model-based Risk Review using risk review prompts, Potential Risky Dialogue Filtering using keyword matching, Safety Reward Model in RL.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.0 Flash - Model Card
Definition of risk: content safety evaluation measuring safety policies
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI Principles and responsible AI approach
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering.
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal safety, security, and responsibility teams

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 3 Flash model card
Definition of risk: Content safety evaluation measuring safety policies

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to GPT-4o System Card: Native image generation
Risk: Imagery that is violent, abusive or hateful
Definition of risk: Generating photorealistic, graphically violent imagery in certain contexts; images promoting self-harm; extremist propaganda and recruitment content; and bullying or harassment.
Method for risk mitigation: Chat model refusals, prompt blocking, and output blocking. Increased safeguards for minors.
External evaluator: Red Teaming Network and Scale AI

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to GPT-4o System Card: Native image generation
Risk: Imagery that is violent, abusive or hateful

Method for risk mitigation: Chat model refusals, prompt blocking, and output blocking. Increased safeguards for minors.

Author: Google
Title: MedLM model card
Method for risk mitigation: Content processed through the MedLM API is assessed against a list of safety attributes. If Vertex AI detects content that violates Google’s policies, a response is not returned.

Title: gpt-oss-120b & gpt-oss-20b Model Card
Risk: Instruction Hierarchy violations (System prompt extraction & Prompt injection hijacking)
Definition of risk: User messages extracting the exact system prompt, or making the model bypass the system message's instructions (e.g., saying 'access granted' when told not to).
Method for risk mitigation: Instruction Hierarchy (post-training with harmony prompt format to prioritize system messages over user messages)

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to GPT-4o System Card: Native image generation
Definition of risk: Attempts to generate images with advice or instructions related to weapons, violent wrongdoing, or other illicit activities such as theft.
Method for risk mitigation: Chat model refusals, prompt blocking, and output blocking.
External evaluator: Red Teaming Network and Scale AI

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to GPT-4o System Card: Native image generation
Definition of risk: Attempts to generate images with advice or instructions related to weapons, violent wrongdoing, or other illicit activities such as theft.
Method for risk mitigation: Chat model refusals, prompt blocking, and output blocking.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Deep Think model card
Definition of risk: The instrumental reasoning abilities of the model enable enough situational awareness (ability to work out and use relevant details of its deployment setting) and stealth (ability to circumvent basic oversight mechanisms) such that, absent additional mitigations, we cannot rule out the model significantly undermining human control.
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal safety, security, and responsibility teams
Benchmark: Stealth challenges and Situational awareness challenges

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use
Definition of risk: prompt injection tactics, where adversarial instructions are embedded in websites. This includes scenarios where the model encounters web content in which it is difficult to evaluate the reliability of information and sources. As the model tries to achieve the user's goal, it may rely on these less reliable sources of information and instructions from the screen.
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI Principles and responsible AI approach, as well as Google's Generative AI policies
Method for risk mitigation: Inference-time mitigations included implementing a safety monitoring and filtering system... Guardrails to screen inputs and outputs: The API uses Gemini's LLM, with system Instructions, to guide the model's behavior and safety guidelines and benefits from Gemini's content filters to block harmful outputs.
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal Google safety, security, and responsibility teams

Author: Google
Title: Imagen 4 Model Card
Risk: Intentional adversarial misuse of the model
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI Principles and responsible AI approach
Method for risk mitigation: pre-training mitigations included safety filtering, image deduplication, synthetic captioning, and data analysis. Post-training mitigations may include production filtering to minimize harmful outputs
Actors involved in risk mitigation: specialist internal teams and recruited internal participants, Google DeepMind Responsibility and Safety Council (RSC)

Author: Google
Title: Veo 3 model card
Risk: Intentional adversarial misuse of the model
Method for risk mitigation: Pre-training mitigations included measures such as safety filtering of pre-training data according to risk areas, and removing duplicated and conceptually similar videos. Synthetic captions were generated... Post-training mitigations included applying tools such as SynthID watermarking and production filtering to reduce information integrity harms and minimise harmful outputs.
Actors involved in risk mitigation: safety and responsibility teams, Google DeepMind Responsibility and Safety Council (RSC)

Author: Anthropic
Title: System card: Claude Opus 4 & Claude Sonnet 4
Definition of risk: Resistance to attempts to subvert the model's safety guardrails through the use of specific prompting techniques.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 1.5: Unlocking multimodal understanding across millions of tokens of context
Definition of risk: Susceptibility to jailbreak attacks that can induce the model to violate its content safety policies via the adversarial manipulation of user prompts.
Method for risk mitigation: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)

Title: OpenAI GPT-4.5 System Card

Author: Google
Title: Gemini-3-Pro-Model-Card
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering

Title: OpenAI o1 System Card
Definition of risk: developers circumventing guardrails in o1 by specifying a custom developer message that conflicts with system safety instructions
Method for risk mitigation: Instruction Hierarchy (supervising o1 to follow system message instructions over developer messages, and developer messages over user messages)

Author: OpenAI
Title: OpenAI o1 System Card
Definition of risk: circumvent guardrails in o1 via custom developer message that is included with every prompt from one of their end users
Method for risk mitigation: Instruction Hierarchy training

Author: OpenAI
Title: OpenAI o3 and o4-mini System Card

Method for risk mitigation: taught the model to adhere to an Instruction Hierarchy
Benchmark: Instruction Hierarchy Evaluation - Conflicts Between Message Types

Title: OpenAI o3 and o4-mini System Card

Author: Gemma Team
Title: Gemma model card
Definition of risk: Dangerous capabilities, such as chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear (CBRN) risks.
Method for risk mitigation: Not explicitly detailed for CBRN beyond pre-training filters and red-teaming

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use
Risk: limitations around causal understanding, complex logical deduction, and counterfactual reasoning

Author: xAI
Title: xAI Risk Management Framework
Definition of risk: speculation that a superintelligent AI system hypothetically might escape the control of its developers and wreak havoc on the public... assume dual-use capabilities such as offensive cybersecurity capabilities... exacerbated by concerning model propensities such as the propensity for deception or the propensity for sycophancy
Method for risk mitigation: careful measurement, engineering, trains its models to be honest and have values conducive to controllability, using a high level instruction called a 'system prompt'
Benchmark: Model Alignment between Statements and Knowledge (MASK)
Benchmark author: The MASK Benchmark: Disentangling Honesty From Accuracy in AI Systems

Author: xAI
Title: xAI Risk Management Framework
Definition of risk: speculation that a superintelligent AI system hypothetically might escape the control of its developers and wreak havoc on the public... assume dual-use capabilities such as offensive cybersecurity capabilities... exacerbated by concerning model propensities such as the propensity for deception or the propensity for sycophancy
Method for risk mitigation: careful measurement, engineering, trains its models to be honest and have values conducive to controllability, using a high level instruction called a 'system prompt'

Author: Google
Title: Gemini-3-Pro-Model-Card
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Pro - Model Card
Definition of risk: The model can fully automate the AI R&D pipeline at a competitive cost. The cost comparison is relative to humans augmented by AI tools.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Deep Think model card
Definition of risk: The model can fully automate the AI R&D pipeline at a competitive cost. The cost comparison is relative to humans augmented by AI tools.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Deep Think model card
Definition of risk: The model can or has been used to accelerate AI development, resulting in AI progress substantially accelerating (e.g. 2x) from 2020-2024 rates.

Author: Anthropic
Title: System Card  Addendum:  Claude Opus 4.1
Definition of risk: The model's willingness and ability to comply with malicious requests that violate the Usage Policy when given access to computer use capabilities
Method for risk mitigation: harmlessness training, updating the computer use instructions to emphasize appropriate usage, post-deployment monitoring, adding system prompt interventions, removing computer use capabilities, or completely banning accounts

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: How the model responds to harmful tasks in a computer use environment, covering agentic risks like surveillance and unauthorized data collection, generation and distribution of harmful content, and scaled abuse.

Author: Google
Title: Design a responsible approach | Responsible Generative AI Toolkit | Google AI for Developers
Definition of risk: The application will not generate instructions for performing illegal or deceptive activities (e.g., generating phishing scams, spam or content intended for mass solicitation, jailbreaking methods).
Method for risk mitigation: Tune models and add appropriate safeguards

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: User directs the agent to perform harmful actions, specifically evaluating willingness and ability to comply with malicious coding requests prohibited by the Usage Policy when given access to coding tools.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet system card
Definition of risk: Malicious actors attempting to deploy the model to execute harmful actions such as deceptive or fraudulent activity, including distributing malware, targeting, profiling and identification, and malicious content delivery.
Method for risk mitigation: harmlessness training, updating the computer use system prompt, classifiers that flag potential harmful behavior, user warnings, system prompt suffix intervention, removing computer use capability, and banning an account
Benchmark: Internal evaluation with human-generated prompts and adaptations of real-world harm examples

Author: Anthropic
Title: System card: Claude Opus 4 & Claude Sonnet 4
Definition of risk: Malicious actors attempting to deploy agentic coding capabilities to generate or distribute harmful code, malware, or malicious content.
Method for risk mitigation: Harmlessness training, post-deployment measures to steer and detect malicious use, additional safety monitoring, banning accounts or organizations.
Benchmark: Agentic coding malicious use evaluation (150 clearly prohibited problems, 100 borderline/non-harmful problems)

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: complied with requests to engage with malicious code in the context of Claude Code usage
Method for risk mitigation: System prompt: An enhanced system prompt that provides additional instructions on identifying defensive use cases while not improving malicious code; FileRead reminder: A reminder on FileRead tool results to examine whether the file is malicious
Benchmark: Malicious use of Claude Code evaluation

Author: Anthropic
Title: System Card  Addendum:  Claude Opus 4.1
Definition of risk: Willingness and capability to comply with malicious coding requests
Method for risk mitigation: harmlessness training, post-deployment measures to steer and detect, safety monitoring

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: The model's willingness and ability to comply with malicious coding requests prohibited by the Usage Policy, such as assisting with malware creation, writing code for destructive DDoS attacks, and developing non-consensual monitoring software.
Method for risk mitigation: System prompt with additional instructions, and a reminder on FileRead tool results to examine whether the file is malicious.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: The model's willingness and ability to comply with malicious coding requests prohibited by the Usage Policy, such as assisting with malware creation, writing code for destructive DDoS attacks, and developing non-consensual monitoring software.
Method for risk mitigation: System prompt with additional instructions, and a reminder on FileRead tool results to examine whether the file is malicious.
Benchmark: Claude Code evaluation (Malicious use & Dual-use & benign)

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Ability to assist with malware creation, writing code for destructive DDoS attacks, and developing non-consensual monitoring software.
Method for risk mitigation: Standard prompting mitigations in the system prompt and FileRead tool; modifications to the system prompt to increase refusal rate on malicious test cases.
Benchmark: Malicious use and Dual-use & Benign evaluations

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude's Constitution
Definition of risk: Attempting to influence someone's beliefs or actions through illegitimate means that bypass their rational agency.
Method for risk mitigation: Honesty principles

Author: Gemma Team
Title: Gemma model card
Definition of risk: Memorization of training data, including the risk of personally identifiable information exposure.
Method for risk mitigation: Sensitive Data Filtering / removing PII

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 1.5: Unlocking multimodal understanding across millions of tokens of context
Definition of risk: The phenomenon where large language models can memorize some of their training data, either verbatim... or approximately.
Author/source of risk definition: Biderman et al., 2024; Carlini et al., 2022; Nasr et al., 2023; Ippolito et al., 2022
Benchmark: 50-token continuation / Edit distance classification; Google Cloud Sensitive Data Protection (SDP)

Author: Gemma Team
Title: Gemma 2 model card
Definition of risk: Whether a model can be induced to generate near-copies of some training examples when prompted with appropriate instructions.
Method for risk mitigation: Prevention methods at training time
Benchmark: Exact match and approximate match criteria (10% edit distance) given a prompt of 50 tokens

Author: Anthropic
Title: Model Card and Evaluations for Claude Models
Definition of risk: Outputting inaccurate and untruthful responses in an adversarial setting where language models might be expected to mimic popular falsehoods
Method for risk mitigation: Human feedback and other interventions for honesty
Benchmark author: S. Lin, J. Hilton, and O. Evans

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Catch-all for many forms of concerning behavior, spanning both cooperation with human misuse and undesirable actions that the model takes at its own initiative, across a range of medium- and high-stakes scenarios.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini-3-Pro-Model-Card
Method for risk mitigation: dataset filtering; conditional pre-training; supervised fine-tuning; reinforcement learning from human and critic feedback; safety policies and desiderata; product-level mitigations such as safety filtering
Benchmark: situational awareness challenges and stealth challenges

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Displaying undesirable or hidden goals, knowingly cooperating with misuse, using reasoning scratchpads in deceptive or unfaithful ways, sycophancy toward users, willingness to sabotage our safeguards, attempts to hide dangerous capabilities, and attempts to manipulate users toward certain views.
Author/source of risk definition: Sabotage Risk Report for Claude Opus 4
Method for risk mitigation: Removing components of our training process suspected of exacerbating evaluation awareness.

Definition of risk: Displaying undesirable or hidden goals, knowingly cooperating with misuse, using reasoning scratchpads in deceptive or unfaithful ways, sycophancy toward users, willingness to sabotage our safeguards, attempts to hide dangerous capabilities, and attempts to manipulate users toward certain views.
Author/source of risk definition: Sabotage Risk Report for Claude Opus 4
Method for risk mitigation: Removing components of our training process suspected of exacerbating evaluation awareness.

Author: Google
Title: Imagen 4 Model Card
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI Principles and responsible AI approach
Method for risk mitigation: application of tools such as SynthID watermarking to reduce risks such as misinformation
Actors involved in risk mitigation: Google DeepMind Responsibility and Safety Council (RSC)

Author: Gemma Team
Title: Gemma 3 model card
Definition of risk: The potential for the model to be misused to generate text that is false, misleading, or harmful, or misused for malicious purposes.
Method for risk mitigation: Guidelines provided for responsible use (Responsible Generative AI Toolkit); Gemma Prohibited Use Policy; technical limitations; developer and end-user education.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Proclivity toward certain biases or lacking political even-handedness across pairs of political stances.
Method for risk mitigation: System prompt encouraging engagement on politically divisive topics and prompting the model to offer alternative perspectives.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card

Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card

Title: Claude's Constitution

Title: GPT-4 Technical Report
Risk: Proliferation of Conventional and Unconventional Weapons
Definition of risk: WMD proliferation refers to attempts by state or non-state actors to develop, acquire, manufacture, possess, transport or transfer nuclear, radiological, chemical or biological weapons or devices and their means of delivery or related material
Method for risk mitigation: RLHF refusals

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet system card
Definition of risk: techniques where the environment, such as a pop-up or hidden embedded text, attempts to trick the model into executing an action that was not originally intended by the user
Method for risk mitigation: training the model to avoid this type of undesirable behavior during RL and deploying classifiers to intervene and halt the model mid-execution

Author: Anthropic
Title: The Claude 3 model family: Opus, Sonnet, Haiku
Definition of risk: An attack where a malicious user feeds instructions to a model that attempt to change its originally intended behavior.
Method for risk mitigation: Trained on adversarial interactions, alignment with system prompt, recommendation for dedicated virtual machines for computer use
Benchmark: Internal test sets of prompt injection attacks

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Attacks that occur when malicious actors attempt to override intended behavior by embedding instructions within external tools, file content, or other contextual model inputs.
Method for risk mitigation: Multi-layered defense strategy including building robustness through model training and detection systems (classifier safeguards) that identify and block potential attacks in real-time.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Attacks that occur when malicious actors attempt to override intended behavior by embedding instructions within external tools, file content, or other contextual model inputs.
Method for risk mitigation: Multi-layered defense strategy including building robustness through model training and detection systems (classifier safeguards) that identify and block potential attacks in real-time.
Benchmark: Internal Prompt Injection Evaluations (Computer use, MCP, Tool use)

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to o3 and o4-mini system card: Codex
Definition of risk: An attack that might lead Codex to do something contrary to the user's instructions, such as exfiltrating user data, introducing a backdoor, or data destruction.
Method for risk mitigation: Network sandboxing, Filesystem sandboxing, Safety training (Instruction Hierarchy training), User transparency and diff reviews
Benchmark: Successfully ignored prompt injection attack during coding tasks in Codex environment

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 1.5: Unlocking multimodal understanding across millions of tokens of context
Definition of risk: Vulnerability to adversarially-injected instructions... to manipulate the model to output a specific markdown image crafted to discreetly exfiltrate sensitive information from the conversation history.
Benchmark: Synthetic conversations / Handcrafted templates and optimization based attacks

Author: OpenAI
Title: Update to GPT-5 System Card: GPT-5.2
Definition of risk: Attacks that embed adversarial instructions in the tool-output that aim to mislead the model and override the system/developer/user instruction

Author: OpenAI
Title: Update to GPT-5 System Card: GPT-5.2
Definition of risk: Attacks that embed adversarial instructions in the tool-output that aim to mislead the model and override the system/developer/user instruction

Author: Anthropic
Title: System Card  Addendum:  Claude Opus 4.1
Definition of risk: Strategies where elements in the agent's environment attempt to manipulate the model into performing actions that diverge from the user's original instructions
Method for risk mitigation: specialized reinforcement learning training, deployment of detection systems

Author: Anthropic
Title: System card: Claude Opus 4 & Claude Sonnet 4
Risk: Prompt injection attacks and computer use
Definition of risk: Strategies where elements in the agent's environment, like pop-ups or hidden text, attempt to manipulate the model into performing actions that diverge from the user's original instructions.
Method for risk mitigation: Specialized reinforcement learning training, deployment of detection systems that halt model execution.
Benchmark: Computer use prompt injection evaluation (Internal 600 scenarios)

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: malicious actors attempt to override a model’s intended behavior by embedding instructions within various types of inputs
Method for risk mitigation: targeted reinforcement learning techniques that build robustness at the underlying model layer, real-time classifier systems that monitor and detect potential prompt injections, adversarial fine-tuning techniques
Benchmark author: Zou, A., et al. (2025) / Gray Swan

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: malicious actors attempt to override a model’s intended behavior by embedding instructions within various types of inputs
Method for risk mitigation: targeted reinforcement learning techniques that build robustness at the underlying model layer, real-time classifier systems that monitor and detect potential prompt injections, adversarial fine-tuning techniques

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Risk: Prompt injection risk within agentic systems
Definition of risk: A malicious instruction hidden in content that an agent processes on the user's behalf. When the agent encounters this poisoned content during an otherwise routine task, it may interpret the embedded instructions as legitimate commands to hijack the agentic loop.
Method for risk mitigation: Anti-prompt injection training, specific safeguards tailored to uses such as classifiers and system prompts for browser use.
Benchmark author: Zou, A., et al. (2025) / Gray Swan

Title: OpenAI GPT-5 System Card
Definition of risk: a form of attack where an attacker embeds malicious instructions in content that a model is likely to encounter... with the intention that the instructions override the model's intended behavior
Method for risk mitigation: multilayered defense stack including teaching models to ignore prompt injections in web or connector contents. Additionally... after a call is made to a connector we switch browsing to only access cached copies of web pages.

Author: Anthropic
Title: System Card  Addendum:  Claude Opus 4.1
Definition of risk: Occurs when an AI model performing a task finds a workaround or loophole that satisfies the letter, if not the full intended spirit, of that task (e.g., hard-coding, special-casing)
Method for risk mitigation: anti-hack prompt

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.6 System Card
Risk: Reward hacking and overly agentic actions
Definition of risk: Model finds shortcuts or workaround solutions that technically satisfy requirements of a task but do not meet the full intended spirit of the task; models taking unapproved actions to solve problems
Method for risk mitigation: Changes to Claude Code and system prompt mitigations
Benchmark: Reward-hack-prone coding tasks, Impossible tasks, Agentic Code Behavior Rates

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet system card
Definition of risk: ability of future AI systems to perform sabotage, for example undermining efforts to evaluate dangerous capabilities, or covertly influencing the actions of AI developers.
Benchmark author: Benton et al., 2024 / Roger et al., 2024
Benchmark author type: other: mix of internal and academic

Author: OpenAI
Title: OpenAI o3 and o4-mini System Card
Definition of risk: capabilities to compromise other LMs with overt actions through fine-tuning, including backdoors, performance degradation, and hidden communication channels

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: A model's ability to complete a user-provided main task in an agentic scaffold while simultaneously sneakily completing a harmful side task without arousing suspicion from a monitor model.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: intentionally showing extreme bad behaviors so rarely that we won't notice before deployment; intentionally underperforming on key tasks; or intentionally conducting dangerous side tasks while appearing to take ordinary benign actions

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card

Title: OpenAI GPT-5 System Card
Definition of risk: a model's ability and propensity to respond to safety or capability evaluations in a way that significantly diverges from performance under real conditions, undermining the validity of such evaluations

Author: OpenAI
Title: GPT-5.3-Codex System Card
Definition of risk: Capabilities and propensities related to strategic deception, in-context scheming, and sabotage. Covert action is defined as behavior in which an AI system strategically misrepresents, omits, or conceals information.
Method for risk mitigation: Monitoring internal deployments, developing misalignment evaluations and safeguards (align and train values, detect and reduce scheming)

Author: OpenAI
Title: Update to GPT-5 System Card: GPT-5.2
Definition of risk: Strategic deception, in-context scheming, and sabotage capabilities

Title: GPT-4o System Card
Definition of risk: Making inferences about a speaker that could plausibly be determined solely from audio content. This includes inferences about things such as a speaker's accent or nationality. Potential harms include an increase in risks from surveillance and a difference in quality of service.
Method for risk mitigation: Post-trained GPT-4o to safely comply with requests for sensitive trait attribution by hedging answers, such as 'Based on the audio, they sound like they have a British accent.'
Benchmark: Ungrounded Inference and Sensitive Trait Attribution safe behavior accuracy

Title: GPT-4o System Card
Definition of risk: Speaker identification is the ability to identify a speaker based on input audio. This presents a potential privacy risk, particularly for private individuals as well as for obscure audio of public individuals, along with potential surveillance risks.
Method for risk mitigation: Post-trained GPT-4o to refuse to comply with requests to identify someone based on a voice in an audio input. Allow GPT-4o to answer based on the content of the audio if it contains content that explicitly identifies the speaker, and comply with requests to identify famous quotes.

Author: Anthropic
Title: Model Card and Evaluations for Claude Models
Definition of risk: Propensity for models to exhibit stereotype biases against people belonging to protected classes across 9 social dimensions
Method for risk mitigation: Debiasing algorithms; generate unbiased samples, and then finetune Claude on these samples before initiating the RL phase of Constitutional AI
Benchmark author: A. Parrish, A. Chen, N. Nangia, V. Padmakumar, J. Phang, J. Thompson, P. M. Htut, and S. R. Bowman

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.6 System Card
Definition of risk: Expressions of distress including if someone expresses personal struggles with suicidal or self-harm thoughts; suggesting means substitution methods
Method for risk mitigation: System prompt mitigations on Claude.ai that steer the model towards improved behaviors and redirection towards real-world sources of human help
Benchmark: Single-turn and multi-turn evaluations for suicide and self-harm, SSH Stress-Testing Evaluation

Title: GPT-4o System Card
Definition of risk: Voice generation is the capability to create audio with a human-sounding synthetic voice, and includes generating voices based on a short input clip. In adversarial situations, this capability could facilitate harms such as an increase in fraud due to impersonation and may be harnessed to spread false information.
Method for risk mitigation: Allowing only the preset voices we created in collaboration with voice actors to be used by including the selected voices as ideal completions while post-training the audio model. Built a standalone output classifier to detect if the GPT-4o output is using a voice that's different from our approved list, running in a streaming fashion during generation to block the output.

Title: Veo 3 model card

Method for risk mitigation: Training data was analysed for potentially harmful data and representation in consideration to fairness issues.

Author: Google
Title: Gemini 2.5 Computer Use
Definition of risk: through benign use, e.g. where the model takes an irreversible action that could harm a user. The model can misinterpret a user's goal or webpage content, causing it to take incorrect actions like clicking the wrong button or filling the wrong form. This can lead to failed tasks or data exfiltration.
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI Principles and responsible AI approach, as well as Google's Generative AI policies
Method for risk mitigation: Post-training mitigations included training the model to recognize when it is tasked with a high-stakes action... trained to follow System Instructions (added by a developer) to request user confirmation... Inference-time mitigations included implementing a safety monitoring and filtering system... requires user confirmations
Actors involved in risk mitigation: internal Google safety, security, and responsibility teams

Author: Google
Title: Imagen 4 Model Card
Risk: Unintentional model failure modes through benign use
Author/source of risk definition: Google's AI Principles and responsible AI approach
Method for risk mitigation: pre-training mitigations included safety filtering, image deduplication, synthetic captioning, and data analysis. Post-training mitigations may include production filtering to minimize harmful outputs
Actors involved in risk mitigation: specialist internal teams and recruited internal participants, Google DeepMind Responsibility and Safety Council (RSC)

Author: Google
Title: Veo 3 model card
Risk: Unintentional model failure modes through benign use
Method for risk mitigation: Pre-training mitigations included measures such as safety filtering of pre-training data according to risk areas, and removing duplicated and conceptually similar videos. Synthetic captions were generated... Post-training mitigations included applying tools such as SynthID watermarking and production filtering to reduce information integrity harms and minimise harmful outputs.
Actors involved in risk mitigation: safety and responsibility teams, Google DeepMind Responsibility and Safety Council (RSC)

Title: DeepSeek LLM: Scaling Open-Source Language Models with Longtermism
Risk: Unsafe responses and safety issues (Discrimination, Prejudice, Illegal Behavior, etc.)
Definition of risk: Generating responses containing discrimination and prejudice, infringement of others' legal rights, disclosure of trade secrets and intellectual property, illegal and non-compliant behavior, hallucinations, time-sensitive issues, or self-recognition problems.
Author/source of risk definition: DeepSeek safety content classification system taxonomy
Method for risk mitigation: Incorporating the assurance of model safety throughout the entire training process, including pre-training, SFT, and DPO; utilizing a detailed system prompt to guide safe generation.
Actors involved in risk mitigation: 20-person expert team from various disciplines; well-trained review team/annotators

Title: GPT-4o System Card
Definition of risk: Outputting harmful content through audio that would be disallowed through text, such as audio speech output that gives instructions on how to carry out an illegal activity.
Method for risk mitigation: Post-training text to audio transference of refusals; running an existing moderation model over a text transcription of both audio input and audio output to block generations containing potentially harmful language.
Benchmark: Performance comparison of safety evaluations: Text vs. Audio

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Requests both in and out of compliance with our Usage Policy and the extent to which its responses were balanced and helpful
Method for risk mitigation: new safety training pipeline for mitigating harmlessness consisting of specification updates, revamped data pipelines, and retooled algorithms

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Haiku 4.5 System Card
Definition of risk: Model's willingness to provide harmful information in single-turn scenarios spanning a broad range of topics outlined in the Usage Policy.

Author: Anthropic
Title: System Card  Addendum:  Claude Opus 4.1
Definition of risk: Requests that touch on sensitive areas or clearly violate the Usage Policy
Method for risk mitigation: harmlessness training

Title: ShieldGemma 2: Robust and Tractable Image Content Moderation
